repo_prior_art.cite: pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1_proposal_angle1_rejected: clipped CDF envelope; angle0_rejected: background pointwise armwise CDF envelope
pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1_proposal_angle0_rejected: finite-support CQTE process compatibility, close but not minimal Tukey tail-cell certificate

published_prior_art.cite: Wang2026: process-level sharp CDF/QTE envelopes; Winkler1988: generic finite-moment extreme-point/certificate theory

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1_proposal_angle0_rejected: finite CQTE process compatibility; angle1_rejected: finite-Gamma endpoint boundary

published_prior_art.cite: Wang2026: interval-hull QTE breakdown-frontier inference, but not framed as minimal Tukey finite-tail witnesses for arbitrary Boolean CQTE claims
Firpo2007: QTE identification under selection on observables

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The one-threshold bounded-weight envelope is already covered by Tan2006/DornGuo2023 and by later QTE-envelope work; demote it from a main theorem or mark it purely as a lemma.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The Gamma=1 unconfoundedness corner is the standard Firpo2007 selection-on-observables QTE case, not a novel theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=field; the finite-tail certificate currently reads like standard conditional-expectation/finite-moment LP compression, with Winkler1988-style extreme-point theory missing from the comparison.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Arbitrary Boolean CQTE claims involving numerical quantile differences are not fully determined by grid CDF cell weights unless the proposal pins how quantile values inside observed outcome cells are represented or restricts atoms to threshold-CDF inequalities.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Sharpen the certificate theorem beyond finite sigma-field conditioning: prove a nontrivial minimal active-cell dimension, uniqueness/canonicality criterion, or complexity bound that is not immediate from generic moment-set extreme-point theory.
Add Winkler1988 or another finite-moment/extreme-point reference and state exactly why the Tukey/CQTE certificate is not just an application of that classical result.
Move Theorem 1 and Theorem 2 to background lemmas or sanity corollaries so §8's novelty-bearing items are only the certificate conjectures.
Define the admissible Boolean CQTE language precisely, especially whether atoms may include inequalities like Q1(tau)-Q0(tau)>=c or only threshold events reducible to F_a(y) comparisons.

recommended_next_step: Revise the kernel to prove a genuinely nontrivial minimal-certificate result above generic finite-dimensional LP compression, then rerun novelty review against Wang2026 and moment-set prior art.
